excerpt: The Creator expressed Himself through the First Intellect (al-ʿAql al-Awwal), who testifies to His divinity. Yet testimony is ordinarily given by one being concerning another, not by the Creator regarding His own essence.
source_location_hint: Section on divine testimony and the nature of creation

excerpt: The Creator brought forth the entirety of existence as pure grace and mercy, seeking neither cause nor compensation. For it lies in the very nature of the Generous and the Powerful to bestow bounty without reason or prerequisite.
The multitude of emanations was so great because there exists no limit to His grace and His bestowal of gifts. This reflects the inexhaustible nature of divine generosity.
source_location_hint: On the number of spiritual beings

excerpt: The simultaneous creation of all beings reflects divine justice. It is as though the rays of the sun illuminate the entire world instantaneously, or as fire kindles every corner of a dark house in a single moment. There was no temporal succession in the spiritual world.
source_location_hint: On the manner of creation of the spiritual world

excerpt: The spiritual world is not a container into which intellects were transferred. Rather, it came into being through the emanation of the intellects themselves. The intellects did not enter a pre-existing realm; from their emanation the world arose.
source_location_hint: On the nature of the spiritual world and intellects

excerpt: All emanations were created by the Almighty in a single manner, from one perspective, at one level, and with one essence. The bestowal of perfection flowed from the grace and mercy of the Creator; indeed, it is the very nature of the Generous that He show mercy.
source_authority: Ismaili doctrine of creation and perfection
source_location_hint: On primary perfection and divine grace

excerpt: All intellects who have attained secondary perfection are constantly increasing in their luminosity. Their knowledge, power, and life are ceaselessly growing, and the bounties of the Mubdiʿ Taʿālā know no limit.
source_location_hint: On the perpetual increase of the illuminated intellects
